main.c(3) Library Functions Manual main.c(3)

NAME

main.c - ascii-chat Unified Binary - Mode Dispatcher and Entry Point

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct mode_descriptor_t
Metadata for a mode.

Macros


#define APP_NAME 'ascii-chat'
#define VERSION ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_FULL
#define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()

Typedefs


typedef int(* mode_entry_point_t) (void)

Functions


atomic_t g_should_exit ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO (g_should_exit)
bool should_exit (void)
void signal_exit (void)
void set_interrupt_callback (void(*cb)(void))
void setup_signal_handlers (void)
int main (int argc, char *argv[])

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Unified Binary - Mode Dispatcher and Entry Point

This file implements the main entry point for the unified ascii-chat binary, which provides server, client, mirror, and discovery service functionality in a single executable.

The dispatcher now delegates ALL option parsing (including mode detection and binary-level options) to options_init(), then simply dispatches to the appropriate mode-specific entry point based on the detected mode.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

Version

3.0

Fix: CMAKE_OSX_SYSROOT properly configured for macOS builds WebRTC: Pass CMAKE_OSX_SYSROOT to subprocess for Threads discovery Test: Running macOS builds with WebRTC Threads fix

Definition in file main.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define APP_NAME 'ascii-chat'

#define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()

Value:

do {                                                                                                                 \


  static bool _global_atomics_registered = false;                                                                    \


  if (!_global_atomics_registered) {                                                                                 \


    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_exit, "application_exit_flag", NULL);                                            \


    _global_atomics_registered = true;                                                                               \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Definition at line 185 of file main.c.


185 #define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()                                                                                      \
186   do {                                                                                                                 \
187     static bool _global_atomics_registered = false;                                                                    \
188     if (!_global_atomics_registered) {                                                                                 \
189       NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_exit, "application_exit_flag", NULL);                                            \
190       _global_atomics_registered = true;                                                                               \
191     }                                                                                                                  \
192   } while (0)

atomic_t g_should_exit ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO
  (g_should_exit )


Global flag indicating application should exit (used by all

References g_should_exit.






int
  main (int argc, char * argv[])


Definition at line 467 of file main.c.


467                                  {
468   // Set global argc/argv for early argv inspection (e.g., in terminal.c)
469   g_argc = argc;
470   g_argv = argv;
471 
472   // Validate basic argument structure
473   if (argc < 1 || argv == NULL || argv[0] == NULL) {
474     return 1;
475   }
476 
477   // Show cursor early in case a previous session crashed with it hidden
478   (void)terminal_cursor_show();
479 
480   // Initialize the named registry for debugging (allows --debug-state to show registered synchronization primitives)
481 #ifndef NDEBUG
482   named_init();
483   // Initialize atomic operations debug tracking
484   debug_atomic_init();
485   // Register global atomics with descriptive names for debug sync state monitoring
486   REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS();
487   // Static atomics are now auto-registered via ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO macro at definition time
488   // Register all packet types from the packet_type_t enum
489   named_registry_register_packet_types();
490 #endif
491 
492   // Register the main thread IMMEDIATELY after named_init() to ensure it's available for all subsequent allocations
493 #ifndef NDEBUG
494 #ifdef _WIN32
495   NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD(GetCurrentThread(), "main", NULL);
496 #else
497   NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD((asciichat_thread_t)asciichat_thread_self(), "main", NULL);
498 #endif
499   // Also save main thread ID for memory reporting (must be very early)
500   debug_sync_set_main_thread_id();
501 #endif
502 
503   // VERY FIRST: Scan for --color BEFORE ANY logging initialization
504   // This sets global flags that persist through cleanup, enabling --color to force colors
505   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
506     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--color=true") == 0) {
507       g_color_flag_passed = true;
508       g_color_flag_value = true;
509       break;
510     } else if (strncmp(argv[i], "--color=", 8) == 0) {
511       g_color_flag_passed = true;
512       g_color_flag_value = strcmp(argv[i], "--color=false") != 0;
513       break;
514     }
515   }
516 
517   // SECOND: Scan for --grep BEFORE ANY logging initialization
518   // This ensures ALL logs (including from shared_init) can be filtered
519   // Supports multiple --grep patterns (ORed together)
520   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
521     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--grep") == 0) {
522       const char *pattern = argv[i + 1];
523       asciichat_error_t filter_result = grep_init(pattern);
524       if (filter_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
525         log_error("Invalid --grep pattern or invalid flags: \"%s\" - use /pattern/flags format (e.g., \"/query/ig\" or \"/literal/F\")", pattern);
526         return 1;
527       }
528       i++; // Skip the pattern argument
529     }
530   }
531 
532   // Detect terminal capabilities early so colored help output works
533   // Logging will be initialized by asciichat_shared_init() before options_init()
534   log_redetect_terminal_capabilities();
535   terminal_capabilities_t caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
536   caps = apply_color_mode_override(caps);
537 
538   // Warn if Release build was built from dirty working tree
539 #if ASCII_CHAT_GIT_IS_DIRTY
540   if (strcmp(ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE, "Release") == 0) {
541     // Dirty git warning will be logged after logging initialization
542   }
543 #endif
544 
545   // Load color scheme early (from config files and CLI) before logging initialization
546   // This allows logging to use the correct colors from the start
547   options_colorscheme_init_early(argc, argv);
548 
549   // Parse --color-scheme from argv early to set logging colors for help output
550   const char *colorscheme_name = "pastel"; // default
551   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
552     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color-scheme") == 0) {
553       colorscheme_name = argv[i + 1];
554       break;
555     }
556   }
557 
558   // Load and apply colorscheme to logging BEFORE options_init() so help gets colors
559   color_scheme_t scheme;
560   if (colorscheme_load_builtin(colorscheme_name, &scheme) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
561     log_set_color_scheme(&scheme);
562   } else {
563     // Fall back to default pastel if scheme not found
564     if (colorscheme_load_builtin("pastel", &scheme) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
565       log_set_color_scheme(&scheme);
566     }
567   }
568 
569   // Initialize logging colors so they're ready for help output
570   log_init_colors();
571 
572   // EARLY PARSE: Find the mode position (first positional argument)
573   // Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
574   int mode_position = -1;
575   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
576     if (argv[i][0] != '-') {
577       // Found a positional argument - this is the mode or session string
578       mode_position = i;
579       break;
580     }
581 
582     // Skip option and its argument if needed
583     // Check if this is an option that takes a required argument
584     const char *arg = argv[i];
585     const char *opt_name = arg;
586     if (arg[0] == '-') {
587       opt_name = arg + (arg[1] == '-' ? 2 : 1);
588     }
589 
590     // Handle --option=value format
591     if (strchr(opt_name, '=')) {
592       continue; // Value is part of this arg, no need to skip next
593     }
594 
595     // Options that take required arguments
596     if (strcmp(arg, "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "-L") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--log-level") == 0 ||
597         strcmp(arg, "--config") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--color-scheme") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--log-template") == 0) {
598       if (i + 1 < argc) {
599         i++; // Skip the argument value
600       }
601     }
602   }
603 
604   // EARLY PARSE: Determine mode from argv to know if this is client-like mode
605   // The first positional argument (after options) is the mode: client, server, mirror, discovery, acds
606   bool is_client_like_mode = false;
607   if (mode_position > 0) {
608     const char *first_arg = argv[mode_position];
609     if (strcmp(first_arg, "client") == 0 || strcmp(first_arg, "mirror") == 0 || strcmp(first_arg, "discovery") == 0) {
610       is_client_like_mode = true;
611     }
612   }
613 
614   // EARLY PARSE: Extract log file from argv (--log-file or -L)
615   // Must appear BEFORE the mode
616   // Generate default log path based on build type and mode
617   char default_log_path[PATH_MAX];
618   asciichat_mode_t detected_mode = MODE_INVALID;
619   if (mode_position > 0) {
620     const char *mode_str = argv[mode_position];
621     // Convert mode string to enum
622     if (strcmp(mode_str, "server") == 0) {
623       detected_mode = MODE_SERVER;
624     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "client") == 0) {
625       detected_mode = MODE_CLIENT;
626     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "mirror") == 0) {
627       detected_mode = MODE_MIRROR;
628     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "acds") == 0 || strcmp(mode_str, "discovery-service") == 0) {
629       detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE;
630     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "discovery") == 0) {
631       detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY;
632     }
633   }
634   generate_default_log_path(detected_mode, default_log_path, sizeof(default_log_path));
635 
636   const char *log_file = default_log_path;
637   int max_search = (mode_position > 0) ? mode_position : argc;
638   for (int i = 1; i < max_search - 1; i++) {
639     if ((strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0)) {
640       log_file = argv[i + 1];
641       break;
642     }
643   }
644 
645   // EARLY PARSE: Check for --json (JSON logging format)
646   // If JSON format is enabled, we'll use the JSON filename during early init
647   // --json MUST appear BEFORE the mode
648   bool early_json_format = false;
649   if (mode_position > 0) {
650     for (int i = 1; i < mode_position; i++) {
651       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--json") == 0) {
652         early_json_format = true;
653         break;
654       }
655     }
656   } else {
657     // If no mode found, search entire argv
658     for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
659       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--json") == 0) {
660         early_json_format = true;
661         break;
662       }
663     }
664   }
665 
666   // EARLY PARSE: Extract log template from argv (--log-template)
667   // Note: This is for format templates, different from --log-format which is the output format
668   // Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
669   const char *early_log_template = NULL;
670   bool early_log_template_console_only = false;
671   for (int i = 1; i < max_search - 1; i++) {
672     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-template") == 0) {
673       early_log_template = argv[i + 1];
674       break;
675     }
676   }
677   for (int i = 1; i < max_search; i++) {
678     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-format-console-only") == 0) {
679       early_log_template_console_only = true;
680       break;
681     }
682   }
683 
684   // Initialize shared subsystems BEFORE options_init()
685   // This ensures options parsing can use properly configured logging with colors
686   // If JSON format is requested, don't write text logs to file
687   // All logs will be JSON formatted later once options_init() runs
688   const char *early_log_file = early_json_format ? NULL : log_file;
689   asciichat_error_t init_result = asciichat_shared_init(early_log_file, is_client_like_mode);
690   if (init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
691     return init_result;
692   }
693 
694   // Route logs to stderr if stdout is piped (MUST happen early, before options_init logs)
695   // This keeps stdout clean for data output (e.g., --snapshot mode piped to file)
696   if (terminal_should_force_stderr()) {
697     log_set_force_stderr(true);
698   }
699 
700   // Register cleanup of shared subsystems to run on normal exit
701   // Library code doesn't call atexit() - the application is responsible
702   // IMPORTANT: atexit handlers run in LIFO order - last registered runs first
703   // So register order (top to bottom) = execution order (bottom to top):
704   // 1. PCRE2 cleanup (runs last) - frees compiled regexes after all code stops using them
705   // 2. Path cleanup (runs 3rd) - now a no-op but kept for API compatibility
706   // 3. Shared destroy (runs 2nd) - frees all shared resources
707   // 4. Cursor show (runs 1st) - shows cursor before exit
708   (void)atexit(asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all);
709   (void)atexit(path_cleanup_thread_locals);
710   (void)atexit(asciichat_shared_destroy);
711   (void)atexit(on_exit_show_cursor);
712 
713   // SECRET: Check for --backtrace (debug builds only) BEFORE options_init()
714   // Prints a backtrace and exits immediately - useful for debugging hangs
715 #ifndef NDEBUG
716   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
717     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--backtrace") == 0) {
718       log_info("=== Backtrace at startup ===");
719       backtrace_print_simple(0);
720       log_info("=== End Backtrace ===");
721       asciichat_shared_destroy();
722       return 0;
723     }
724   }
725 #endif
726 
727   // NOW parse all options - can use logging with colors!
728   asciichat_error_t options_result = options_init(argc, argv);
729   if (options_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
730     asciichat_error_context_t error_ctx;
731     if (HAS_ERRNO(&error_ctx)) {
732       log_error("Error: %s", error_ctx.context_message);
733     } else {
734       log_error("Error: Failed to initialize options (error code: %d)", options_result);
735     }
736 
737     // Clean up options state before exiting
738     options_state_destroy();
739     options_cleanup_schema();
740 
741     exit(options_result);
742   }
743 
744   // Get parsed options
745   const options_t *opts = options_get();
746   if (!opts) {
747     log_error("Error: Options not initialized");
748     return 1;
749   }
750 
751   // Determine final log file path (use mode-specific default from options if available)
752   // Determine output format early to decide on filename and logging strategy
753   bool use_json_logging = GET_OPTION(json);
754 
755   // Determine the log filename
756   // Check if user explicitly passed --log-file (not just the mode-specific default from options_init)
757   bool user_specified_log_file = false;
758   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
759     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0) {
760       user_specified_log_file = true;
761       break;
762     }
763   }
764 
765   const char *final_log_file = opts->log_file;
766   char json_filename_buf[256];
767 
768   if (use_json_logging) {
769     // When JSON logging is enabled, determine JSON output filename
770     if (user_specified_log_file) {
771       // User explicitly specified a log file - use it exactly for JSON output
772       SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
773     } else {
774       // Using default: replace .log with .json in the mode-specific default
775       // e.g., server.log -> server.json, mirror.log -> mirror.json, etc.
776       size_t len = strlen(final_log_file);
777       if (len > 4 && strcmp(&final_log_file[len - 4], ".log") == 0) {
778         // File ends with .log - replace with .json
779         SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
780         // Replace .log with .json
781         asciichat_error_t strcpy_result = SAFE_STRCPY(&json_filename_buf[len - 4],
782                                                        sizeof(json_filename_buf) - (len - 4), ".json");
783         if (strcpy_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
784           log_error("Failed to write .json extension: %s", asciichat_error_string(strcpy_result));
785         }
786       } else {
787         // File doesn't end with .log - just append .json
788         SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
789         strncat(json_filename_buf, ".json", sizeof(json_filename_buf) - strlen(json_filename_buf) - 1);
790       }
791     }
792     final_log_file = json_filename_buf;
793     // For JSON mode: Initialize logging without file (text will be disabled)
794     // We'll set up JSON output separately below
795     // Note: log_init() was already called in asciichat_shared_init()
796   } else {
797     // Text logging mode: use the file from options (which is mode-specific default or user-specified)
798     // Note: log_init() was already called in asciichat_shared_init()
799   }
800 
801   // Apply custom log template if specified (use early parsed value if available, otherwise use options)
802   const char *final_format = early_log_template ? early_log_template : GET_OPTION(log_template);
803   bool final_format_console_only =
804       early_log_template ? early_log_template_console_only : GET_OPTION(log_format_console_only);
805   if (final_format && final_format[0] != '\0') {
806     asciichat_error_t fmt_result = log_set_format(final_format, final_format_console_only);
807     if (fmt_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
808       log_error("Failed to apply custom log format");
809     }
810   }
811 
812   // Configure JSON output if requested
813   if (use_json_logging) {
814     // Open the JSON file for output
815     int json_fd = platform_open("main_log_file", final_log_file, O_CREAT | O_RDWR | O_TRUNC, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE);
816     if (json_fd >= 0) {
817       log_set_json_output(json_fd);
818     } else {
819       // Failed to open JSON file - report error and exit
820       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to open JSON output file: %s", final_log_file);
821       return ERROR_CONFIG;
822     }
823   }
824 
825   // Initialize colors now that logging is fully initialized
826   // This must happen after log_init() since log_init_colors() checks if g_log.initialized
827   log_init_colors();
828 
829   // Apply quiet mode - disables terminal output
830   // Status screen mode only disables terminal output if terminal is interactive
831   // In non-interactive mode (piped output), logs go to stdout/stderr normally
832   if (GET_OPTION(quiet) ||
833       (opts->detected_mode == MODE_SERVER && GET_OPTION(status_screen) && terminal_is_interactive())) {
834     log_set_terminal_output(false);
835   }
836 
837   // Initialize palette based on command line options
838   const char *custom_chars = opts && opts->palette_custom_set ? opts->palette_custom : NULL;
839   if (apply_palette_config(GET_OPTION(palette_type), custom_chars) != 0) {
840     FATAL(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to apply palette configuration");
841   }
842 
843   // Set quiet mode for memory debugging
844 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY) && !defined(USE_MIMALLOC_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
845   debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(GET_OPTION(quiet));
846 #endif
847 
848   // Truncate log if it's already too large
849   log_truncate_if_large();
850   fflush(stderr);
851 
852   // Handle --help and --version (these are detected and flagged by options_init)
853   // Terminal capabilities already initialized before options_init() at startup
854   if (opts->help) {
855     print_usage(opts->detected_mode);
856     fflush(NULL);
857     _Exit(0);
858   }
859 
860   if (opts->version) {
861     log_set_terminal_output(true);
862     action_show_version();
863     // action_show_version() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
864   }
865 
866 #ifndef NDEBUG
867   // Handle --debug-state (debug builds only)
868   // Sleep for specified time AFTER mode initialization so locks are created
869   if (IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT(debug_sync_state_time, opts) && opts->debug_sync_state_time > 0.0) {
870     log_info("Will print sync state after %f seconds", opts->debug_sync_state_time);
871   }
872 #endif
873 
874   // For server mode with status screen: disable terminal output only if interactive
875   // In non-interactive mode (piped output), logs go to stdout/stderr normally
876   // The status screen (when shown) will capture and display logs in its buffer instead
877   if (opts->detected_mode == MODE_SERVER && opts->status_screen && terminal_is_interactive()) {
878     log_set_terminal_output(false);
879   }
880 
881   log_dev("Logging initialized to %s", final_log_file);
882 
883   // Note: We do NOT auto-disable colors when stdout appears to be piped, because:
884   // 1. Tools like ripgrep can display ANSI colors when piped
885   // 2. Sandboxed/containerized environments may report false positives for isatty()
886   // 3. Users can explicitly disable colors with --color=false if needed
887   // Color behavior is now fully controlled by --color and --color-mode options.
888 
889 #ifndef NDEBUG
890   // Initialize lock debugging system after logging is fully set up
891   log_debug("Initializing lock debug system...");
892   int debug_sync_result = debug_sync_init();
893   if (debug_sync_result != 0) {
894     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Debug sync system initialization failed");
895     FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Debug sync system initialization failed");
896   }
897   log_debug("Debug sync system initialized successfully");
898 
899   // Initialize memory debug system
900   // TEMPORARILY DISABLED: these calls hang in non-interactive mode
901   // log_debug("Initializing memory debug system...");
902   // int debug_memory_result = debug_memory_thread_init();
903   // if (debug_memory_result != 0) {
904   //   LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Memory debug system initialization failed");
905   //   FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Memory debug system initialization failed");
906   // }
907   // log_debug("Memory debug system initialized successfully");
908 
909   // Start memory debug (now just prints report directly)
910   // if (debug_memory_thread_start() != 0) {
911   //   LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Memory debug startup failed");
912   //   FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Memory debug startup failed");
913   // }
914 
915 #ifndef _WIN32
916   // Unblock SIGUSR1 and SIGUSR2 at process level to ensure delivery
917   sigset_t set;
918   sigemptyset(&set);
919   sigaddset(&set, SIGUSR1);
920   sigaddset(&set, SIGUSR2);
921   sigprocmask(SIG_UNBLOCK, &set, NULL);
922 
923   // Register SIGUSR1 to trigger synchronization debugging output in all modes
924   signal_handler_t old_usr1 = platform_signal(SIGUSR1, common_handle_sigusr1);
925   if (old_usr1 == SIG_ERR) {
926     log_warn("Failed to register SIGUSR1 handler");
927   } else {
928     log_debug("SIGUSR1 handler registered successfully");
929   }
930 
931   // Register SIGUSR2 to trigger memory report in all modes
932   signal_handler_t old_usr2 = platform_signal(SIGUSR2, common_handle_sigusr2);
933   if (old_usr2 == SIG_ERR) {
934     log_warn("Failed to register SIGUSR2 handler");
935   } else {
936     log_debug("SIGUSR2 handler registered successfully");
937   }
938 #endif
939 #endif
940 
941   if (opts->fps > 0) {
942     if (opts->fps < 1 || opts->fps > 144) {
943       log_warn("FPS value %d out of range (1-144), using default", opts->fps);
944     } else {
945       log_debug("FPS set from command line: %d", opts->fps);
946     }
947   }
948 
949   // Automatic update check at startup (once per week maximum)
950   if (!GET_OPTION(no_check_update)) {
951     update_check_result_t update_result;
952     asciichat_error_t update_err = update_check_startup(&update_result);
953     if (update_err == ASCIICHAT_OK && update_result.update_available) {
954       char notification[1024];
955       update_check_format_notification(&update_result, notification, sizeof(notification));
956       log_info("%s", notification);
957 
958       // Set update notification for splash/status screens
959       splash_set_update_notification(notification);
960     }
961   }
962 
963   // Set up global signal handlers BEFORE mode dispatch
964   // All modes use the same centralized exit mechanism
965   setup_signal_handlers();
966 
967   // Register application callbacks so lib code can check exit flags
968   // This connects the render loop's should_exit check to signal_exit()
969   static const app_callbacks_t app_callbacks = {
970       .should_exit = should_exit,
971       .signal_exit = signal_exit,
972       .platform_get_monotonic_time_us = platform_get_monotonic_time_us,
973       .platform_sleep_ms = platform_sleep_ms,
974   };
975   app_callbacks_register(&app_callbacks);
976 
977   // Register shutdown callback so splash thread and other code can check for exit
978   shutdown_register_callback((shutdown_check_fn)should_exit);
979 
980 #ifndef NDEBUG
981   // Start debug threads now, after initialization but before mode entry
982   // This avoids lock contention during critical initialization phase
983   // Note: debug_sync_start_thread() and debug_memory_thread_start() now use
984   // direct pthread calls instead of mutex_init/cond_init to avoid named registry
985   // deadlock during thread startup
986   if (debug_sync_start_thread() != 0) {
987     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Debug sync thread startup failed");
988     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Debug sync thread startup failed");
989   }
990   log_debug("Debug sync thread started");
991 
992 #endif
993 
994   // Find and dispatch to mode entry point
995   const mode_descriptor_t *mode = find_mode(opts->detected_mode);
996   if (!mode) {
997     log_error("Error: Mode not found for detected_mode=%d", opts->detected_mode);
998     return 1;
999   }
1000 
1001   // Call the mode-specific entry point
1002   // Mode entry points use options_get() to access parsed options
1003   int exit_code = mode->entry_point();
1004 
1005   // Named registry cleanup is handled by asciichat_shared_destroy()
1006   // (called via atexit handler), which runs AFTER the memory report
1007 
1008   if (exit_code == ERROR_USAGE) {
1009     exit(ERROR_USAGE);
1010   }
1011 
1012   return exit_code;
1013 }






References action_show_version(),
    app_callbacks_register(), apply_color_mode_override(),
    apply_palette_config(), ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all(),
    asciichat_shared_destroy(), asciichat_shared_init(),
    asciichat_thread_self(), backtrace_print_simple(),
    colorscheme_load_builtin(),
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    debug_atomic_init(), debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(),
    debug_sync_init(), debug_sync_set_main_thread_id(),
    debug_sync_start_thread(),
    options_state::debug_sync_state_time,
    detect_terminal_capabilities(), options_state::detected_mode,
    mode_descriptor_t::entry_point, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, ERROR_THREAD, ERROR_USAGE,
    FATAL, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE, options_state::fps,
    g_argc, g_argv, g_color_flag_passed,
    g_color_flag_value, GET_OPTION, grep_init(),
    HAS_ERRNO, options_state::help, IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT,
    log_debug, log_dev, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error,
    log_file, options_state::log_file, log_info,
    log_init_colors(), log_redetect_terminal_capabilities(),
    log_set_color_scheme(), log_set_force_stderr(),
    log_set_format(), log_set_json_output(),
    log_set_terminal_output(), log_truncate_if_large(),
    log_warn, MODE_CLIENT, MODE_DISCOVERY,
    MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, MODE_INVALID, MODE_MIRROR,
    MODE_SERVER, named_init(), NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD,
    named_registry_register_packet_types(),
    options_cleanup_schema(), options_colorscheme_init_early(),
    options_get(), options_init(), options_state_destroy(),
    options_state::palette_custom,
    options_state::palette_custom_set,
    path_cleanup_thread_locals(),
    platform_get_monotonic_time_us(), platform_open(),
    platform_signal(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS, SAFE_STRCPY, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    SET_ERRNO, setup_signal_handlers(), should_exit(),
    shutdown_register_callback(), signal_exit(),
    splash_set_update_notification(),
    options_state::status_screen, terminal_cursor_show(),
    terminal_is_interactive(), terminal_should_force_stderr(),
    update_check_result_t::update_available,
    update_check_format_notification(), update_check_startup(),
    and options_state::version.






void
  set_interrupt_callback (void(* cb )(void))


Register a mode-specific interrupt callback




Called by mode-specific code to register a callback that will be
    invoked when signal_exit() is called. Used to shut down network
    sockets so threads blocked in recv() unblock quickly instead of waiting for
    timeouts.




The callback is called synchronously from signal_exit(), so
    it must be async-signal-safe (only atomics, socket_shutdown(), no
    malloc/etc).




Only one callback can be registered at a time. Setting a new
    callback replaces the previous one.




Parameters


cb Function to call on exit signal, or NULL to
  unregister


Definition at line 222 of file main.c.


222                                               {
223   g_interrupt_callback = cb;
224 }










void
  setup_signal_handlers (void )


Set up global signal handlers Called once at startup before mode
    dispatch


Definition at line 341 of file main.c.


341                                  {
342   platform_set_console_ctrl_handler(console_ctrl_handler);
343 
344 #ifndef _WIN32
345   platform_signal_handler_t handlers[] = {
346       {SIGINT, handle_sigterm},
347       {SIGTERM, handle_sigterm},
348       {SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN},
349   };
350   platform_register_signal_handlers(handlers, 3);
351   log_debug("Signal handlers registered");
352 #endif
353 }






References log_debug,
    platform_register_signal_handlers(), and
    platform_set_console_ctrl_handler().


Referenced by main().






bool should_exit (void )


Check if the application should exit




Called by mode main loops to detect shutdown requests from signals
    or errors.




Returns


true if shutdown has been requested, false
  otherwise


Definition at line 206 of file main.c.


206                        {
207   return atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit);
208 }






References atomic_load_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.






void
  signal_exit (void )


Signal that the application should exit




Sets the global exit flag and calls the registered interrupt
    callback if set. Safe to call from signal handlers (uses only atomics and
    function pointers). Called by signal handlers (SIGTERM, Ctrl+C) and normal
    code (errors, timeouts).


Definition at line 210 of file main.c.


210                        {
211   // Note: This function may be called from a signal handler context where
212   // other threads may hold mutex locks. We avoid log_console() here to prevent
213   // deadlock (render loop holds terminal lock, signal handler can't acquire it).
214   // The shutdown will be logged by normal thread context later.
215   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
216   void (*cb)(void) = g_interrupt_callback;
217   if (cb) {
218     cb();
219   }
220 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.19
    ascii-chat
